### Post by Jeroenverh on 2006-06-14
Hi there,

Last weekend I installed Ubuntu 6.06. The default resolution was 1280X1024 at 60 hz. Now for the last few days I tried to change that to 75 hz. I tried the options given on the page  "HOWTO: change resolution/refresh rate in Xorg"  but nothing works.

Is there someone who wants to take a look at my xorg.conf file?

Here it is:

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

### Post by ajgreeny on 2006-06-14
The system only uses the plain xorg.conf file, all the rest are just backups with the date and time they were made in the figures at the end.

As to your refresh rate, I'm a bit baffled as you seem to have the horizontal and vertical figures in the file and they should support that rate of 75 that you can't get.  Perhaps someone else knows more about this than I do, or perhaps the modelines are not quite what is needed  I've never quite understood this modeline business but others do use it and add various other modelines to their xorg.conf file.  There is even a web site that will produce modelines for you if you enter all the info about your machine and monitor so give this site a try, but back everything up first.

[http://www.dkfz-heidelberg.de/spec/linux/modeline/](http://www.dkfz-heidelberg.de/spec/linux/modeline/)

Good luck.

---

### Post by blastus on 2006-06-14
First off, your custom modeline looks incorrect. At the standard 1280x1024 75hz VESA mode, the pixel clock rate should calculate to 138.00 not 138.54. You need to look up the documentation for your monitor and find out exactly what its "preset timing modes" are. A preset timing mode specifies a resolution, vertical frequency, horizontal frequency, pixel clock, and sync polarity.

Secondly, your custom modeline is not being referenced properly in the Screen section. Since you've labelled it "1280x1024_75.00" it should be referenced as "1280x1024_75.00" NOT "1280X1024" in the Screen section. 

Thirdly, if you using DFP (an LCD), you need to specify the AllowNon60HzDFPModes option and most likely the ExactModeTimingsDVI option (if you are using a DVI cable.)

I have the same model card you have FX5600. See this thread here for more info:
[NVIDIA: How I fixed my display resolution problem with DVI-D connection!](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=194531)

---

### Post by Mark Rose on 2006-06-16
I have a similar problem, that being refresh stuck at 60 hz when using the nvidia driver. It's an issue with the most recent update from nvidia. If you go back a version, things seem to work according to people.

---

### Post by blastus on 2006-06-17
[QUOTE=Jeroenverh]I see that there are more people who have a similar problem like me.
I browsed the ubuntuforums and I haven't found an solution for my problem. I also see that others haven't been able to solve similar problems.
Does anyone know what the problem with X Windows is? 
Why it is not possible to set the refresh rate at 75 hz. ?

Thanks in advance[/QUOTE]

It should be possible to get a 1280x1024 75hz refresh rate, but in order to get help you need to provide some info. Are you able to run at that resolution/refresh rate in Windows? Are you using a CRT or an LCD? Are you using a D-Sub, DVI-I, or DVI-D cable? What are your monitor specs? What does the EDID of your monitor report? (look in /var/log/Xorg.0.log) 

I've already mentioned at least one reason why your xorg.conf file won't work. Your custom modeline is not being referenced properly in the Screen section because you've labelled it "1280x1024_75.00" but you are referencing it as "1280x1024" Also, have you read this [NVIDIA page](http://download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86/1.0-8762/README/appendix-d.html)? It covers all the driver options that the NVIDIA driver supports.

---

### Post by walterius on 2006-06-17
No matter how many times I set my refresh rate to 75 Hz (a 1024x768), Ubuntu set it back to 60 Hz at each reboot. I fixed it in the Screen Resolution Preferences by setting the refresh rate to 75 Hz and then X-ing the box that said "Make default for this computer (<username>) only." It's been fine ever since.

Dunno if that will help, but it allowed me to avoid editing files altogether.

---
